data.status: anticipates being customer ready at some point in May
data.short_description: 1902 is the latest concept from Alora Restaurant Group, serving American fare in a 1,600 sq ft storefront. The restaurant recently received its certificate of occupancy and no official opening date has been announced, though a May opening is anticipated. Chef Amy DiBiase serves as Culinary Director and COO.

Magdaleña is a Latin-American wood-fired kitchen opening at Goodyear's GSQ Civic and Cultural district, led by executive chef Jason Alford and bar manager Gilles Kolakowski. The menu focuses on premium meats, fresh seafood, and vegetable-forward dishes, with a wine and cocktail program sourced exclusively from Latin America. The dining room is designed by Phoenix-based House of Form.

data.status: Tentative opening is looking like late summer right now
data.short_description: South Block, a fast-casual acai bowl and smoothie chain, is opening its 7th DC location and 26th overall in the former Robeks space in Tenleytown. The founder confirmed a tentative late summer opening timeline to What Now DC. The menu will include acai bowls, smoothie bowls, smoothies, toasts, and juices.

data.store_name: Robeks Fruit Smoothies and Healthy Eats
data.short_description: Robeks Fruit Smoothies and Healthy Eats closed its Tenleytown location in August 2025. The space will be taken over by South Block. No further details on the closure are provided in the article.

Scott's Fat Cats LLC applied for permits with the Florida Department of Business and Professional Regulation to open Fat Cats Billiards at 306 W. Ardice Avenue in Eustis. The venue will offer 19 pool tables, four dart boards, a full bar, and a menu of appetizers, with family-friendly hours and 21+ late-night hours. No official opening date has been announced.

Co-owners Lisa Mondello and Bulent "Ben" Cicek, founders of Meaux Jeaux Pizza and Istanbul Grill, are launching Upper Crust Pizzeria & Italian Cuisine in the CBD. The flagship concept will focus on house-made Italian focaccia for traditional Sicilian sandwiches in a 3,145 sq ft interior with a 750 sq ft courtyard. No opening date has been announced.

data.status: opening a restaurant at 110 Alhambra Circle, with a target opening date of late May
data.short_description: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza, known for star-shaped pizzas using 72–96 hour fermented dough, is opening its 10th corporate-owned location in Coral Gables. The brand was founded in 2014 by Umberto Mascagni and chef Renato Viola and has grown to approximately 30 locations across Florida, Texas, and Georgia.

Sourdough Bread Company is preparing to open a new location in the Fifth Street Plaza shopping center in Davis, operated by Tracy-based owner Nav Nijjar. The fast-casual concept currently operates six locations across Northern California and features fresh-baked sourdough bread and nearly 20 sandwiches. No official opening date has been announced.

Chef John Currence's Big Bad Breakfast has signed a lease at 113 E. Main St. in Murfreesboro, the former home of City Cafe. The Southern-inspired breakfast-and-lunch chain now has 29 locations across nine states. What Now Nashville is awaiting updates on the opening timeline.

A liquor license application has been submitted for ForeLand Beer to open a brewery at 240 N Broadway in Portland's Lloyd District. The listed operating hours are 3–10 p.m. Monday–Friday and 12–10 p.m. on weekends, with seating for 20 indoors and 16 outdoors. Managing Partner David Sanguinetti was not immediately available for comment.

data.event_date: Possibly mid-May or June (year not specified)
data.status: opening is expected in "(possibly) mid-May or June"
data.short_description: Family-owned Niños Birrieria, which currently operates two food trucks, is planning to open a brick-and-mortar restaurant in Wilsonville. No address has been revealed yet. The menu will include birria dishes, quesa tacos, and Birria Ramen, along with beer and cocktails.

data.status: actively pursuing expansion into the area
data.short_description: Makenna Koffee, a multi-location cafe brand, is exploring a new El Cajon location at 1275 Broadway, currently home to Cafe Bella, based on permit filings with the San Diego Department of Environmental Health and Quality. No official opening date has been announced and no owners or managers have publicly commented. The brand currently operates locations in Simi Valley, Valencia, Ventura, Camarillo, and Westlake Village.

data.location: 1867 Yonge Street, Toronto (zip code not specified)
data.status: planned to open before the 2026 World Cup, which starts in June
data.short_description: Blue Horse Eatery, a modern Italian bar concept, is opening at 1867 Yonge Street in the former home of Fionn MacCool's in Toronto. Led by Christian, who closed Kramer's Bar & Grill in summer 2025, the restaurant will offer casual Italian bar dining including small plates, shareable snacks, and drinks.

data.location: 554 Parliament Street, Toronto (zip code not specified)
data.short_description: Blue Horse Rotisserie, a slow-cooked meat and comfort food concept, is planned for 554 Parliament Street in Toronto, a space currently under renovation. The restaurant will focus on hearty meals including slow-cooked meats, seasonal pastas, salads, sandwiches, and weekend brunch. It is part of a broader plan to open three new restaurants within a year of Kramer's Bar & Grill closure.

data.location: 11200 Lakeline Mall Dr, Cedar Park, TX 78613
data.status: "We're not 100% on the opening yet, but we're thinking May or June"
data.short_description: Halftime Sports Bar & Grill is preparing to open at Lakeline Mall, featuring a chef-driven menu, beer boots, and what is described as "Austin's ONLY Courtside LED Wall." Co-owners Ramon Kreher and Jeremy Tracy confirmed the opening, with a menu including specialty pizzas, burgers, cheese curds, and a 34x48-inch rectangular pizza.

data.location: 378 Technology Center Way, Rock Hill, SC 29730
data.event_date: Later this month (month/year not specified in article)
data.short_description: Mug and Maple is a health-centric coffee shop opening at 378 Technology Center Way in The Power House building in Rock Hill, founded by Nathan Kingsley. The shop will feature natural ingredients, signature maple and honey lattes, and a food menu including breakfast and Italian sandwiches, with no seed oils or artificial ingredients. The space is designed to evoke an upscale ski lodge atmosphere with a 35-foot granite bar.

data.event_date: Construction expected June (year not specified) – April 2027
data.status: construction was expected to start in June and run until April 2027
data.short_description: Karaz Restaurant is a new project from restaurateur Sam Hellman-Mass, led by executive chef and partner Alejandra Kuri, focusing on Middle Eastern flavors with Texas sourcing. The menu will feature wood-fired pita, hummus, spreads, and charcoal-grilled kebabs. A filing with the Texas Department of Licensing & Regulation listed an estimated construction cost of $1 million.

data.event_date: Late fall or early winter (year not specified)
data.status: will launch at 4808 Williams Drive in late fall or early winter
data.short_description: Bojangles has announced a late fall or early winter opening timeline for its Georgetown location at 4808 Williams Drive, following its January 20 Manor location opening. The Southern-style fast food franchise, founded in 1977 in North Carolina, now has more than 800 locations nationwide.

data.location: 2401 N Ponce De Leon Blvd, Suite B, St. Augustine, FL 32084
data.short_description: Maison Louis Café is a French-inspired cafe bringing coffee, croissants, and fresh-baked bread sandwiches to St. Augustine, led by chef Jerome Bensimon. The concept draws inspiration from Gilded Age architecture and prioritizes quality food and guest experience. No official opening date has been announced.

data.location: 577 E Sunny Dunes Rd, Palm Springs, CA 92264
data.status: plan to open in late December 2026
data.short_description: Joe Coffee, a cafe and bakery originally from Provincetown, Massachusetts, is opening its West Coast debut location in Palm Springs at 577 East Sunny Dunes Road. Owners Mark Shaw and Glenn Siegmund plan to replicate the Provincetown menu with West Coast-inspired additions, featuring coffee from Indigo Roasters and vegan/gluten-free options. The cafe will be dog-friendly, kid-friendly, and LGBTQ-inclusive.

data.store_name: HMSHost / Miami International Airport Dining (20+ outlets)
data.status: A timeline for the changes has not been announced
data.short_description: Miami International Airport has entered into a 12-year contract extension with HMSHost to reimagine over 20 restaurants, bars, and grab-and-go outlets across its North, Central, and South terminals. The agreement is part of MIA's $9 billion capital improvement program, with plans to introduce local restaurants, global brands, and digitized dining options. No timeline for the changes has been announced.

data.status: signed a lease to replace Hooters
data.short_description: Supra Revolving Sushi Bar, a New Jersey-born AYCE sushi brand, has signed a lease and submitted plan review paperwork to open at 4521 Southside Blvd, replacing the Hooters that closed abruptly in mid-2025. The restaurant features a revolving conveyor belt system with nigiri, special rolls, hand rolls, and hot hibachi entrées. Owner Min Lu was not immediately available for comment on the opening timeline.

data.status: closed abruptly in the middle of 2025
data.short_description: The Hooters location at 4521 Southside Blvd, which had been open since 2007, closed abruptly in mid-2025. The space has since been leased by Supra Revolving Sushi Bar. No further details on the closure are provided in the article.

data.status: closed abruptly in the middle of 2025
data.short_description: The Hooters location at 4521 Southside Blvd, which had been open since 2007, closed abruptly in mid-2025. The space has since been leased by Supra Revolving Sushi Bar. No further details on the closure are provided in the article.

data.location: 1530 Ellsworth Industrial Blvd NW, Atlanta, GA 30318
data.short_description: Pie Bar is opening a new cafe location at Westside Market in Atlanta, with owner Dominie Garcia targeting a May opening. The new location will offer pies by the slice, take-and-bake savory pies, and espresso beverages. Customers can also pre-order pies for pickup.

data.location: 420 Citi Centre St, Winter Haven, FL 33880
data.short_description: T.Land Boba Tea Lounge is opening at 420 Citi Centre St in Winter Haven, based on plan review documents submitted by T.Land LLC. The concept appears designed as a lounge-style boba tea destination rather than a quick-service stop. No opening date or additional details were available at the time of publication.

Pura Vida Miami is set to open at The Palms at Town & Country in Kendall this summer, in a 3,425 sq ft space. The Miami-born lifestyle brand offers chef-driven foods, artisanal coffee, matcha, and clothing. It was founded in 2012 by Omer Horev and now has over 40 locations.

data.status: scheduled to reopen later this year
data.short_description: Chop Suey Lounge & Ginger Roots is set to reopen at 2877 University Avenue following a $2 million restoration of the historic Chop Suey Peking Cafe, which originally opened in 1931. The 3,500 sq ft venue will offer craft cocktails, live entertainment, and elevated Asian-fusion cuisine across two distinct spaces. Founding partners Jacquelyn Kelly and Jason Bess are leading the project.

Temple Coffee Roasters teased a new ninth location on social media, with locals and a local realtor pointing to the Hazel Ridge Plaza in Fair Oaks at 4301 Hazel Ave. No official address or opening date has been confirmed by the company. The brand was founded in 2005 and currently operates eight cafés across the Sacramento region.

data.location: 57 Yorkville Ave, Toronto, ON M5R 1B7, Canada
data.short_description: Stock TC, a luxury grocery and Italian restaurant concept, is set to open at 57 Yorkville Ave in Toronto, taking over the former Pusateri's space that closed in 2024. The Yorkville location will feature a grocery section, Stock Grille bistro, a Stock Bar, and a Grocer Kitchen. It was founded as a collaboration between Cosimo Mammoliti and Stephen Alexander.

Conrad's Casual Dining is opening its fifth location at The Village Shoppes in Canton, in a 5,636 sq ft space that previously housed Nosh & Grog and Bertucci's. The family-owned chain, founded in 1999 by Bob Conrad, offers American and Italian comfort classics with a full bar and private function room. No official opening date has been announced.

data.location: 78015 Main St, Suite 108, La Quinta, CA 92253
data.short_description: Gabino's Creperie is opening its third location in Old Town La Quinta at 78015 Main St, Suite 108, in the former home of Disco Rabbit. The Coachella Valley cafe began as a pop-up and currently operates in Indio and Palm Springs. No official opening date has been announced.

data.location: Address not specified (Denver Metropolitan Area, Colorado)
data.short_description: Mukja Food Truck, known for Asian fusion snacks, teased a new Denver storefront on social media but has not revealed the address or opening timeline. The truck was founded in 2019 by Julia Rivera and Kayla Makowski, and won first place on Food Network's Food Truck Prize Fight in 2023. Specific details and the opening date are still being finalized.

data.location: 1202 S Idaho St, La Habra, CA 90631
data.short_description: ROL Hand Roll Bar, a fast casual sushi concept from Kei Concepts, is opening its fourth location at 1202 S. Idaho Street in La Habra's Imperial Promenade, in the former Round Table Pizza space. The menu features hand rolls, maki sets, rice bowls, and appetizers. No official opening date has been announced.

Array 36, a modern Chinese fine dining restaurant, is opening its second location at The District at Tustin Legacy, expanding beyond its original San Gabriel location at 5449 Rosemead Blvd. Owner Xinze (Arthur) Zhang is bringing the concept to Tustin with a menu featuring roast duck, sous-vide beef ribs, dim sum, and more. No official opening date has been announced.

Burger Syndicate is a new burger restaurant from the team behind Nacheaux, opening soon at 221 SW Ankeny St in Portland. The menu features loaded fries with slow-smoked brisket, house-made slaw, and other elevated toppings. An exact launch date has not been announced; the Instagram page indicates a spring opening.

data.location: 3833 NE Martin Luther King Jr Blvd, Portland, OR 97212
data.short_description: Fabbt Ethiopia Restaurant is planning to open at 3833 NE Martin Luther King Jr. Blvd in Portland, targeting a May opening according to owner Yafet. The restaurant will serve traditional Ethiopian cuisine including teff-flour injera, stews, lentils, and meats, with seating for about 50 guests. The menu is still under development.

Killer Burger is planning a new location at 11910 US-290, Suite 124 in Manor, TX, with an estimated $900,000 construction project running April 6 to July 1, per a TDLR filing. Founder TJ Southard confirmed the brand is also under construction at a North Austin location at 3500 E Parmer Lane. The chain serves ten burger varieties, chicken sandwiches, milkshakes, and vegetarian options.

data.store_name: Killer Burger (North Austin / East Village location)
data.location: 3500 E Parmer Lane, Austin, TX (zip not specified)
data.short_description: Killer Burger is under construction at a North Austin location at 3500 E Parmer Lane, in the East Village area. Founder TJ Southard confirmed construction is underway as the brand continues to expand. No opening date was specified for this location.

data.status: conversion and renovation (opening date not announced)
data.short_description: The Standard boutique hotel brand is converting and renovating the former South Congress Hotel at 1603 S Congress Ave in Austin, marking the brand's first U.S. opening in more than a decade. The project is a collaboration between Hyatt and Timberline Real Estate Partners, with interior design by Goodrich Design. Plans for the hotel's restaurant portfolio have not yet been revealed.

data.store_name: The Cake Lounge (Crazy Cakes By Becky)
data.location: 1000 S Norman C. Francis Pkwy, Suite 100, New Orleans, LA 70125
data.short_description: The Cake Lounge, a new brick-and-mortar dessert lounge from Crazy Cakes By Becky, is planned for 1000 S. Norman C. Francis Pkwy, Suite 100 in New Orleans, in the former Gracious Bakery location that closed in 2023. Owner Rebecca Broussard has run a dessert catering business since 2015 and is now expanding into a permanent retail space. The menu will include macarons, cookies, café-style food, and non-alcoholic beverages.

data.location: 12553 Bartram Park Blvd, Unit 103, Jacksonville, FL 32258
data.short_description: Lucky Goat Coffee Co., a café franchise with locations in Florida, Georgia, and Texas, appears to be opening a new Jacksonville location at 12553 Bartram Park Blvd, Unit 103, based on plan review documents. The brand focuses on accessible, expertly crafted coffee including espresso drinks, cold brew, and single-origin coffee. No official opening date has been announced.

The Daily Brew Station, a mobile coffee cafe operated by a mother-daughter duo, is opening its first brick-and-mortar location at 551 17th St W in Palmetto. The tentative grand opening is set for April 25, with a buildout currently underway. The menu will include pastries, bagels, waffles, breakfast sandwiches, lunch items, and coffee from Banyan Coffee Co.

Soup Zen Hot Pot, a Canada-based hot pot concept currently operating in Montreal, has received town approval to open its first U.S. location at Burlington Mall in Burlington, MA. The restaurant features an interactive, chef-prepared hot pot experience with a pork-free, Halal-friendly menu drawing from Hong Kong, Japanese, Korean, and Thai influences. Guests can choose from over 100 ingredients, 10 soup bases, and a sauce bar.

data.location: 1834 Westheimer Rd, Houston, TX (zip not specified)
data.short_description: Carlo is a new casual Italian concept from Brasserie 19 owner Charles Clark, opening this fall at 1834 Westheimer Rd in the former Paulie's space in Houston's Montrose neighborhood. The menu will include pastas, pizzas, and table-side dishes, with chef Michael Hoffman running the kitchen. Paulie's closed after failing to reach an agreement with its landlord after 27 years of service.

data.location: 1830 Westheimer Rd, Houston, TX (zip not specified)
data.short_description: Casa Carlo is a new upscale Italian concept from Charles Clark, opening this fall at 1830 Westheimer Rd in the former Camerata space in Houston's Montrose neighborhood. Italian-born chef Davide Deangelis will run the kitchen, with a menu offering pastas, pizzas, and table-side dishes. Both Carlo and Casa Carlo will pay homage to Clark's previous concepts, including the beloved Paulie's cookies.
